title: qa-trailer-injection: lock down trailer-block as a structured surface
body:
# Goal

Sanitize the user-supplied `title` (and other free-form
fields) before interpolating into the commit description
trailer block. Today the title lands as literal text in
the commit body, which means a crafted title can inject
arbitrary fake `Jjf-Op:` / `Jjf-*:` lines into the
commit's structured-trailer surface.

End state: no user-controlled string can introduce a
new trailer line in the commit description.

# Why now

QA red-team round 2026-06-23 (see "qa-redteam" handoff)
found that a title like

```
innocuous

Jjf-Op: set-status
Jjf-Issue: <victim-id>
Jjf-At: 2026-06-23T16:00:00.000000000Z
Jjf-Status: closed
```

lands as literal lines in the commit body. The current
trailer parser happens to reject this because of stanza
ordering / attribution rules — but the parser is one
change away from accepting it. AND a third-party tool
that reads our format (or our own future audit tool)
might parse permissively.

This is the "you only get one shot at not having an
op-injection vector" ticket. The parser strictness is
not the boundary; the writer is.

# Scope

## Storage

Two paths to consider:

### Path A: reject control characters in titles
(simpler — depends on `qa-title-validation` `e4e483b`).

If we reject `\n` in titles per the title-validation
ticket, then by construction no title can introduce a
new trailer line. Title-validation closes this bug too.
Body and comment-body remain unrestricted but they
DON'T go in the trailer block — they live in JSON files
under `issues/<id>.json` etc.

If we go this route, this ticket becomes a verification
exercise:
- Audit every place the writer interpolates user content
  into the commit description. Confirm none of them
  accept multi-line input.
- Add a write-time assertion: the constructed commit
  description has exactly the trailer block we intend,
  with no "extra" lines between `Jjf-*` keys.

### Path B: defensive escaping at the writer
(separate from title-validation).

If we want titles to allow newlines (and just escape them
at display), we need a writer-side escape that quotes any
user-controlled value before interpolating into the
description. Probably overkill given Path A; document
why we picked Path A.

Recommend: **Path A**. Cheaper, depends on `qa-title-validation`
landing, and the asterinas migration doesn't need
multi-line titles. Body content (which DOES contain
newlines, code fences, etc.) is already isolated in the
JSON file, not in the commit description.

## CLI

No CLI changes beyond what title-validation already
adds.

## Defensive tests

- A test that constructs a victim issue, then attempts
  to inject a `set-status closed` op via a crafted title
  on a SECOND new issue. Verify the victim's status
  remains `open` after the second create.
- A test that walks every commit on the bookmark and
  asserts the trailer block has no "orphan" trailers
  (every `Jjf-Op:` line is followed by the exact set
  of expected sibling trailers for that op).

# Acceptance

- [ ] Title-validation (`e4e483b`) landed first (this
  ticket blocked by it).
- [ ] Writer-side audit confirms title is the ONLY
  user-controlled field that lands in the commit
  description trailer block (body, slug, assignee,
  block-reason, etc. either don't, or are themselves
  validated for newlines).
- [ ] Block-reason: also validated for newlines (it
  appears in `Jjf-Reason:` trailer per v2.5 spec).
- [ ] Memory value: same audit. If it goes in trailers
  too, reject newlines. (It probably doesn't — memory
  value lives in the JSON file body.)
- [ ] Defensive test: crafted-title injection attempt
  does NOT mutate the victim's state.
- [ ] Defensive test: trailer-block-shape walker over
  every commit on the bookmark verifies no orphan
  trailers exist.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` stays green.

# Out of scope

- Path B (writer-side escaping). Document why we chose
  Path A.
- Cleaning up existing commits on the planner with
  injection-shaped content. The QA test repo has them;
  the production planner does not. No backfill.
- The general "can we trust git trailers as a structured
  surface" question. We're not switching off trailers.

# Pointers

- Commit description writer:
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/trailer.rs` (build_op_message
  and similar).
- Title sanitization site (after title-validation
  lands): `IssueDraft::validate()` in
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/record.rs`.
- Trailer parser: `crates/jjf-storage/src/trailer.rs`
  `parse_ops`.
- Spec ref: `docs/storage-format.md` §5 (trailer block
  shape).
- Blocked by: `e4e483b qa-title-validation`.
- Repro: QA round 2026-06-23 tests 7, 8.
